papers.title: How We Validate Research at The Encoded Human Project
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence can fabricate with complete confidence. Not occasionally. Not rarely. Structurally. A large language model has no internal mechanism that distinguishes confirmed retrieval from pattern-assembled plausibility. This document describes the validation architecture The Encoded Human Project uses to catch errors before they reach the reader — covering five error types, multi-agent review pipelines, epistemic ceiling labeling, and the standards that govern every claim Pearl produces.

papers.title: Light Machine Translation Key — Conduction: Lipids and Cardiovascular Particles
papers.abstract: The standard lipid panel represents the foundational cardiovascular risk assessment tool in clinical practice, providing measurements of total cholesterol (TC), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), and triglycerides (TG). Despite its ubiquity, its interpretive limits are substantial and must be stated plainly at the outset of any serious discussion of lipid-based cardiovascular risk stratification.

papers.title: Light Machine Translation Key — Regulation: HPG and Metabolic Cluster
papers.abstract: The hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis is not merely an endocrine relay. It is a primary organizing architecture of the human body—a verticalized signal cascade that integrates reproductive readiness with metabolic state, stress load, circadian timing, and energetic sufficiency. To study it clinically is to enter one of the most densely encoded systems in human physiology: a system where the body's decision to reproduce is inseparable from its assessment of whether survival conditions are adequate to sustain new life. That assessment is metabolic. It is nutritional. It is inflammatory.
